* [Aziz Dheri](/wiki/Aziz_Dheri "Aziz Dheri") * [Baho Dheri](/wiki/Baho_Dheri "Baho Dheri") * [Bhamala Stupa](/wiki/Bhamala_Stupa "Bhamala Stupa") * [Jaulian](/wiki/Jaulian "Jaulian") * [Mansehra Rock Edicts](/wiki/Mansehra_Rock_Edicts "Mansehra Rock Edicts") * [Ranigat](/wiki/Ranigat "Ranigat") * [Rehman Dheri](/wiki/Rehman_Dheri "Rehman Dheri") * [Seri Bahlol](/wiki/Seri_Bahlol "Seri Bahlol") * [Sirsukh](/wiki/Sirsukh "Sirsukh") * [Takht-i-Bahi](/wiki/Takht-i-Bahi "Takht-i-Bahi") * [Mian Khan](/wiki/Mian_Khan "Mian Khan")
* [House of Dilip Kumar, Peshawar](/wiki/House_of_Dilip_Kumar,_Peshawar "House of Dilip Kumar, Peshawar") * [Kapoor Haveli](/wiki/Kapoor_Haveli "Kapoor Haveli")

([Pashto](/wiki/Pashto "Pashto") and [Urdu](/wiki/Urdu "Urdu"): باب خیبر; transl. 'Khyber Gate')[[1]](#cite_note-trib-1) is a monument situated at the entrance of the [Khyber Pass](/wiki/Khyber_Pass "Khyber Pass") in the [Khyber Pakhtunkhwa](/wiki/Khyber_Pakhtunkhwa "Khyber Pakhtunkhwa") province of [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan"). The gate is located immediately west of [Peshawar](/wiki/Peshawar "Peshawar"), with the historic [Jamrud Fort](/wiki/Jamrud_Fort "Jamrud Fort") lying adjacent to it.[[1]](#cite_note-trib-1)
Habib, Yusra (20 August 2020). ["This guy travelled to all the landmarks on Pakistani currency notes"](https://images.dawn.com/news/1185674). *Images*. Retrieved 4 June 2024.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Monument in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan

Bab-e-Khyber  
(Khyber Gate)

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| باب خیبر | |
| The Khyber Gate, in April 2014 | |

| Location | [Khyber Pass](/wiki/Khyber_Pass "Khyber Pass"), [Jamrud Subdivision](/wiki/Jamrud_Subdivision "Jamrud Subdivision"), [Khyber Pakhtunkhwa](/wiki/Khyber_Pakhtunkhwa "Khyber Pakhtunkhwa"), [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan") |
| Coordinates | [34°00′09″N 71°22′48″E﻿ / ﻿34.0025°N 71.3800°E﻿ / 34.0025; 71.3800](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Bab-e-Khyber&params=34.0025_N_71.38_E_type:landmark_region:PK) |
| Builder | [Government of Pakistan](/wiki/Government_of_Pakistan "Government of Pakistan") |

| Completion date | 1963 |

The **Bab-e-Khyber** ([Pashto](/wiki/Pashto "Pashto") and [Urdu](/wiki/Urdu "Urdu"): باب خیبر; transl. 'Khyber Gate')[[1]](#cite_note-trib-1) is a monument situated at the entrance of the [Khyber Pass](/wiki/Khyber_Pass "Khyber Pass") in the [Khyber Pakhtunkhwa](/wiki/Khyber_Pakhtunkhwa "Khyber Pakhtunkhwa") province of [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan"). The gate is located immediately west of [Peshawar](/wiki/Peshawar "Peshawar"), with the historic [Jamrud Fort](/wiki/Jamrud_Fort "Jamrud Fort") lying adjacent to it.[[1]](#cite_note-trib-1)

## Gate introduction

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bab-e-Khyber&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Gate introduction")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a4/2007_08_27_Pakistan_Khyber_Pass_locals_commemoration_plaque_IMG_9943.jpg/250px-2007_08_27_Pakistan_Khyber_Pass_locals_commemoration_plaque_IMG_9943.jpg)](/wiki/File:2007_08_27_Pakistan_Khyber_Pass_locals_commemoration_plaque_IMG_9943.jpg)

Plaque commemorating the monument's inauguration in 1963.

The gate was inaugurated by field marshal [Ayub Khan](/wiki/Ayub_Khan_(President_of_Pakistan) "Ayub Khan (President of Pakistan)") on 11 June 1963. Khyber Gate is considered to be the most famous post-independence structure in [Khyber Agency](/wiki/Khyber_Agency "Khyber Agency").

## Pakistani Banknote

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bab-e-Khyber&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Pakistani Banknote")]

Vignette of Khyber Pass, Peshawar, along with the name appear on the 10 Rupees [Pakistani banknote](/wiki/Pakistani_rupee "Pakistani rupee").[[2]](#cite_note-2)
